phase-scope.phase-29: Postgres-backed MTProto assignment registry implemented and verified locally.

V-M-042.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

verification-levels.module-local: Repository and model tests use isolated DB sessions and deterministic fixtures; no live Telegram, DNS, or host mutation.
verification-levels.wave-level: Runs with V-M-043 and migration tests so provisioning cannot persist data the registry cannot restore.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-29 cannot pass unless assignment persistence is enough to rebuild owner-safe MTProto payloads after backup/restore.

module-checks.check-1: Assignment table enforces one canonical active assignment per user.
module-checks.check-2: SNI uniqueness and lifecycle status transitions are deterministic.
module-checks.check-3: Alembic migration is compatible with SQLModel metadata and existing DB tests.
module-checks.check-4: Persisted state excludes raw tg://proxy links, raw base secret, raw salt, and derived secret unless encrypted storage is explicitly approved.
module-checks.check-5: A restore-like fixture can rebuild the public response from user_id, SNI, credential mode, assignment status, and rotation marker.

scenarios.scenario-1: Assignment is saved and read back by user id.
scenarios.scenario-2: Restore-like fixture preserves user linkage, SNI, credential mode, and status.
scenarios.scenario-3: Idempotent save for the same verified user returns the existing canonical assignment without changing SNI.
scenarios.scenario-4: Duplicate SNI or duplicate active user assignment is rejected or converted into an explicit superseded state.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unverified, inactive, missing, or deleted users cannot receive active assignments.
scenarios.scenario-6: Migration drift between SQLModel metadata and Alembic revision fails the gate.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Admin list and repository logs do not contain full tg://proxy links or raw secrets.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Uniqueness and migration errors may include assignment id and user id, but not SNI-derived secret material.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-29 M-042 can be executed autonomously only against local test databases and Alembic metadata checks; production migration apply remains operator-controlled.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: V-M-042 target tests passed locally inside the combined Phase-29 pytest run.
